pages.raw_text:
In reviewing the commercial progress
of the city of Spokane it becomes the
evident duty of the writer to cease, for
a time, the contemplation of splendors
wrought in the wilderness by the hand
of men, and to turn from the consider
alton of sordid achievements to pay a
tribute to the pioneer history-maker
who first blazed the trail to this west
ern Eldorado, and whose brave deeds
of the early days will outlive time it.
self. There dwells in Spokane a pleas
ant, silver-haired gentleman who has
spent quarter of century in the city
and who in spite of his years is still
a potent factor in the great business
enterprises of the west He was one
of the makers of the state constitution.
ane one of the makers of Spokane, and
for that matter of several other cities
in the vicinity. This vigorous captain
of industry has lent a hand to every
civic and business enterprise of any i'm
portance that has been projected since
he first cast his lot with the few pio-
neers encamped at Spokane Falls. He
has seen the rise of Spokane from the
